+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KPEMA771EPB8V51SPKAE0PBB
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: d780
+title: 'Commands: add per_entity flag + emit_cross_cutting_commands mechanism; migrate ui.inspect as pilot'
+---
+## What
+
+Build the single mechanism that makes a cross-cutting command declare itself once and auto-emit on every entity moniker in scope. Then prove it works by retiring the six duplicated `ui.inspect` blocks from entity schemas — without losing the "Inspect <Type>" context-menu item on any entity.
+
+### Mechanism design
+
+1. Add a boolean field `per_entity` (default `false`) to `CommandDef` — the registry struct that backs `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/*.yaml`. Check `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` for the exact struct; add the field with a `#[serde(default)]` so existing YAML is backward compatible.
+
+2. In `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`, add a new pass `emit_cross_cutting_commands`:
+   - Iterate every entity moniker in `scope_chain` (skip `field:*` the same way `emit_scoped_commands` does).
+   - For each registry command with `per_entity: true`, emit a `ResolvedCommand` with `target: Some(entity_moniker)`, templated name resolved with that entity type, and `context_menu` / `keys` inherited from the declaration.
+   - Dedup via the existing `(id, target)` seen set — a command emits at most once per target.
+   - Check `available()` via `check_available(cmd_id, scope_chain, Some(moniker), ...)` so Rust-level opt-outs still filter correctly.
+
+3. Wire `emit_cross_cutting_commands` into `commands_for_scope` between `emit_scoped_commands` and `emit_global_registry_commands`. Document the ordering in a module comment: scoped schema → cross-cutting → scoped registry → global registry → dynamic.
+
+4. Pilot migration — `ui.inspect`:
+   - Update `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`:
+     ```yaml
+     - id: ui.inspect
+       name: "Inspect {{entity.type}}"
+       per_entity: true
+       context_menu: true
+       params:
+         - name: moniker
+           from: target
+     ```
+     Remove `visible: false` — it's no longer hidden.
+   - Delete the `ui.inspect` entries from all six entity YAMLs: `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `board.yaml`, `actor.yaml`.
+   - Confirm `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs::InspectCmd` reads the target moniker and produces the right inspector state.
+
+### Files to touch
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` (or wherever `CommandDef` is defined).
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` — new function + wiring + unit tests.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `board.yaml`, `actor.yaml` — delete six `ui.inspect` entries.
+
+### Subtasks
+
+- [ ] Add `per_entity` to `CommandDef` with serde default.
+- [ ] Implement `emit_cross_cutting_commands` and wire into `commands_for_scope`.
+- [ ] Promote `ui.inspect` in `ui.yaml` and set `per_entity: true`.
+- [ ] Delete the six `ui.inspect` entity schema entries.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `CommandDef` has a `per_entity: bool` field, default `false`, loaded from YAML.
+- [ ] `emit_cross_cutting_commands` exists and is called once per `commands_for_scope` invocation.
+- [ ] Right-click on every entity type (task, tag, project, column, board, actor) still shows an "Inspect <Type>" item that opens the inspector.
+- [ ] `grep -n 'id: ui\\.inspect' swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/` returns zero matches.
+- [ ] `ui.inspect` appears exactly once in the command registry.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Add `per_entity_flag_parses_from_yaml` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` tests: YAML with `per_entity: true` loads to `CommandDef { per_entity: true, .. }`; YAML without the key defaults to `false`.
+- [ ] Add `ui_inspect_auto_emits_on_every_entity_type` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests — parameterized over `["task:01X", "tag:01T", "project:backend", "column:todo", "board:main", "actor:alice"]`. Assert each scope produces a `ResolvedCommand` with `id == "ui.inspect"` and `target == Some(moniker)`.
+- [ ] Add `ui_inspect_dedupes_across_entity_monikers_in_scope` — scope `["task:01X", "column:todo", "board:main"]` emits `ui.inspect` three times (once per target), never duplicated for the same target.
+- [ ] Add `cross_cutting_respects_available_opt_out` — a stub command with `per_entity: true` whose `available()` returns false for `attachment:*` does NOT emit for attachment monikers.
+- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands -p swissarmyhammer-kanban scope_commands` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write `ui_inspect_auto_emits_on_every_entity_type` first; it fails until the mechanism exists AND the entity YAMLs drop their opt-ins.
+
+#commands
+
+Depends on: 01KPEMA771EPB8V51SPKAE0PBB (scope pins must be gone so the cross-cutting pass can emit without fighting scope matching)
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Commands: add per_entity flag + emit_cross_cutting_commands mechanism; migrate ui.inspect as pilot

## What

Build the single mechanism that makes a cross-cutting command declare itself once and auto-emit on every entity moniker in scope. Then prove it works by retiring the six duplicated `ui.inspect` blocks from entity schemas — without losing the "Inspect <Type>" context-menu item on any entity.

### Mechanism design

1. Add a boolean field `per_entity` (default `false`) to `CommandDef` — the registry struct that backs `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/*.yaml`. Check `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` for the exact struct; add the field with a `#[serde(default)]` so existing YAML is backward compatible.

2. In `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`, add a new pass `emit_cross_cutting_commands`:
   - Iterate every entity moniker in `scope_chain` (skip `field:*` the same way `emit_scoped_commands` does).
   - For each registry command with `per_entity: true`, emit a `ResolvedCommand` with `target: Some(entity_moniker)`, templated name resolved with that entity type, and `context_menu` / `keys` inherited from the declaration.
   - Dedup via the existing `(id, target)` seen set — a command emits at most once per target.
   - Check `available()` via `check_available(cmd_id, scope_chain, Some(moniker), ...)` so Rust-level opt-outs still filter correctly.

3. Wire `emit_cross_cutting_commands` into `commands_for_scope` between `emit_scoped_commands` and `emit_global_registry_commands`. Document the ordering in a module comment: scoped schema → cross-cutting → scoped registry → global registry → dynamic.

4. Pilot migration — `ui.inspect`:
   - Update `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`:
     ```yaml
     - id: ui.inspect
       name: "Inspect {{entity.type}}"
       per_entity: true
       context_menu: true
       params:
         - name: moniker
           from: target
     ```
     Remove `visible: false` — it's no longer hidden.
   - Delete the `ui.inspect` entries from all six entity YAMLs: `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `board.yaml`, `actor.yaml`.
   - Confirm `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs::InspectCmd` reads the target moniker and produces the right inspector state.

### Files to touch

- `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` (or wherever `CommandDef` is defined).
- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`.
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` — new function + wiring + unit tests.
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `board.yaml`, `actor.yaml` — delete six `ui.inspect` entries.

### Subtasks

- [ ] Add `per_entity` to `CommandDef` with serde default.
- [ ] Implement `emit_cross_cutting_commands` and wire into `commands_for_scope`.
- [ ] Promote `ui.inspect` in `ui.yaml` and set `per_entity: true`.
- [ ] Delete the six `ui.inspect` entity schema entries.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `CommandDef` has a `per_entity: bool` field, default `false`, loaded from YAML.
- [ ] `emit_cross_cutting_commands` exists and is called once per `commands_for_scope` invocation.
- [ ] Right-click on every entity type (task, tag, project, column, board, actor) still shows an "Inspect <Type>" item that opens the inspector.
- [ ] `grep -n 'id: ui\\.inspect' swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/` returns zero matches.
- [ ] `ui.inspect` appears exactly once in the command registry.

## Tests

- [ ] Add `per_entity_flag_parses_from_yaml` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` tests: YAML with `per_entity: true` loads to `CommandDef { per_entity: true, .. }`; YAML without the key defaults to `false`.
- [ ] Add `ui_inspect_auto_emits_on_every_entity_type` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests — parameterized over `["task:01X", "tag:01T", "project:backend", "column:todo", "board:main", "actor:alice"]`. Assert each scope produces a `ResolvedCommand` with `id == "ui.inspect"` and `target == Some(moniker)`.
- [ ] Add `ui_inspect_dedupes_across_entity_monikers_in_scope` — scope `["task:01X", "column:todo", "board:main"]` emits `ui.inspect` three times (once per target), never duplicated for the same target.
- [ ] Add `cross_cutting_respects_available_opt_out` — a stub command with `per_entity: true` whose `available()` returns false for `attachment:*` does NOT emit for attachment monikers.
- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands -p swissarmyhammer-kanban scope_commands` — all green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write `ui_inspect_auto_emits_on_every_entity_type` first; it fails until the mechanism exists AND the entity YAMLs drop their opt-ins.

+The existing `emit_scoped_commands` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` walks the scope chain and for each entity moniker runs two passes: entity-schema commands and scoped-registry commands. That's exactly the right shape — the new cross-cutting pass slots alongside them.

-- `ParamSource::Target` already existed in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs`; no schema change needed.
